message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Given the Covid-19’ sudden rapid spread in the community since 25 July 2020, an updated analysis of Covid-19 cases was conducted to examine the differences of characteristics of Covid-19 patients before versus after partial lockdown end in Vietnam.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Data of 569 Covid-19 patients confirmed SARS-CoV-2 infection from 23 January to 31 July was collected from available official databases. We divided Covid-19 situation timeline into two main periods, before lockdown end (23 January – 22 April) and after lockdown end (23 April – 31 July).</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>We found significant variations in the distribution of Covid-19 patients among different provinces between two periods. Covid-19 confirmed patients were older in the time after lockdown end compared to in the period before lockdown end by a median of 5 years. All discharged Covid-19 patients and no Covid-19 death were in the phase before lockdown, while post lockdown period had still remained significant patients being under treatment, especially reported first fatalities. The number of Covid-19 patients who returned from other countries (excluding China) slightly increased through two stages (p >0.05), partially showing that the continuous volume of people returning Vietnam from abroad during the Covid-19 epidemic.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Our analysis indicated demographic and epidemiological disparity of Covid-19 patients before versus after loosening the national partial lockdown in Vietnam. It is important to suggest that, proactive efforts in Covid-19 control after partial lockdown end will be effective when the measures to closely control and monitor repatriation and immigration via the borders of Vietnam are strictly enforced.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>What is already known on this topic</jats:title>
                  <jats:list list-type="bullet">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Most of Covid-19 confirmed patients in Vietnam were acquired overseas.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>
                        In Vietnam, Covid-19 epidemic had the low estimated reproduction ratio (R
                        <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                        ) and SARS-CoV-2 spread was on the downward trend before July 25.
                      </jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>What this study adds</jats:title>
                  <jats:list list-type="bullet">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Covid-19 confirmed patients were older in the time after lockdown end compared to in the period before lockdown end by a median of 5 years.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>All discharged Covid-19 patients and no Covid-19 death were in the phase before lockdown, while post lockdown period had still remained significant patients being under treatment, especially reported first fatalities.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Continuous volume of people returning Vietnam from abroad through two stages during the Covid-19 epidemic.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Our analysis might be considered as a prompt reference source for further in-depth surveys to understand the adaptive models of Covid-19 patients among different provinces.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:sec>
title: A Comparison of Covid-19 Patient Characteristics Before versus After Partial Lockdown in Vietnam
